zh.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essex是William de Saye的女兒，於大約1169年出生。她於大約1175年與Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1197年4月19日之前在英國英格蘭諾福爾可，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可守爾達哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Say
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

en.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essex was born about 1169 as the daughter of William de Saye. She married Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex about 1175. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 19 April 1197, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Shouldham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essex est née environ 1169, fille de William de Saye. Elle a épousé Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex environ 1175. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 19 avril 1197, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Shouldham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essex wurde um 1169, als Tochter von William de Saye geboren. Sie hat um 1175 Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 19. April 1197 in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Shouldham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essexは約1169年にWilliam de Sayeの娘として生まれた。約1175年にGeoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essexと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1197年4月19日以前にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクで死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクショウルドハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essex(은)는 대략 1169년 William de Saye의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1175년 Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1197년 4월 19일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 쇼우드함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essex nasceu aproximadamente 1169 filha de William de Saye. Ela casou-se com Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex aproximadamente 1175. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 19 de abril de 1197, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Shouldham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Beatrice Say Countess of Essex родилась около 1169 г. Её отца звали William de Saye. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex около 1175 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 19 апрель 1197 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности шоулдхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.
